channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.123
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.123 (Insiders) Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: Build, deploy, and govern sovereign AI with Foundry Local on Azure Local
item.description: Not every AI workload can run in the cloud. For many of our customers, data needs to stay within defined boundaries, connectivity may be limited or absent, and latency, governance, and auditability are non-negotiable. With Foundry Local on Azure Local, you can use the same model catalog, developer...
item.dc:creator: insagiv

item.title: Designing Cloud Landing Zones by Traffic Flow: A Defence‑in‑Depth, DMZ‑First Architecture
item.description: As enterprises adopt Microsoft Azure for large‑scale and regulated workloads, security architecture must be driven by traffic trust boundaries and inspection intent, not connectivity alone. Regulatory frameworks consistently require a clear separation between Internet‑untrusted and private...
item.dc:creator: AvanishYadav

item.title: Token economics–driven architecture: hybrid models, AI Runway, AKS Kata MicroVM, MCP
item.description: 1. The moment the bill arrived For most of 2024 and 2025, "Agents" were a demo word. In 2026 they are a line item on the cloud invoice. Every major model provider — OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral, DeepSeek, and even the in-cluster open-weights serving stacks — now bills by the token. Input...
item.dc:creator: kinfey

item.title: GitHub Action for Deploying Hosted Agents
item.description: Introduction With Microsoft's introduction to Hosted Agents comes a next logical question. How to implement this? Organizations need a method that is quick, repeatable, and requires minimal adjustments to their existing tooling and processes. Thus, we will walk through how to deploy a Hosted Agent...
item.dc:creator: j_folberth

item.title: New Capabilities to Observe Agents in Azure Monitor
item.description: Over the last six months, we have been listening to you and building new capabilities to help you observe your agents. You’ve been sharing with us that quality issues are tricky and evaluation is critical, that agent reasoning needs to be understood, that humans must be in the loop to review select...
item.dc:creator: MattMc

item.title: Azure SRE Agent at Microsoft Build 2026: Bringing agentic operations to the enterprise
item.description: Build 2026 Update When we launched Azure SRE Agent, the promise was simple: reduce operational toil, improve up time, and evolve teams from manual incident response towards AI-powered operations. Since GA in March 2026, that promise has held up in production. Teams are using the agent to diagnose...
item.dc:creator: dchelupati

item.title: Preinstall to persistence: Inside the Red Hat npm Miasma credential-stealing campaign
item.description: A large-scale npm supply chain attack compromised over 90 versions of @redhat-cloud-services packages, silently infecting CI/CD environments and developer systems. The malicious code steals credentials from GitHub, cloud platforms, and local machines, then spreads like a worm by republishing...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: Deploying Foundry Hosted Agents via REST API
item.description: Introduction At Microsoft Build `26 it was announced that Microsoft Foundry Hosted will soon be generally available. This blog post will outline the deployment process and requirements for creating hosted agents in Foundry. The audience for this blog is designed to range from beginner to more...
item.dc:creator: j_folberth

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Simple log alerts in Azure Monitor
item.description: We are excited to announce the General Availability of Simple log alerts in Azure Monitor, available starting today. This feature is designed to provide a simplified and more intuitive experience for monitoring and alerting, enhancing your ability to dete
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: GPT-4.1 deprecated
item.description: We have deprecated GPT-4.1 across all GitHub Copilot experiences (including Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions), June 1, 2026. Model Deprecation date Suggested alternative GPT-4.1… The post GPT-4.1 deprecated appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Code-first observability for Foundry Agents in VS Code
item.description: Microsoft Foundry brings code-first observability to agent developers in public preview, delivered as an observe skill inside the Foundry Plugin for VS Code Copilot Chat. Developers run the full evaluation-driven optimization loop from the editor through
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: VNet integration for Azure SRE Agent (preview)
item.description: For many production systems, the logs, databases, private endpoints, repositories, and runbooks an SRE Agent needs to do its job are behind network boundaries your security team already governs. VNet integration for Azure SRE Agent, now in preview, puts the agent's outbound traffic under those same...
item.dc:creator: sanchitmehta

item.title: The end of patching era for containers: Microsoft Defender for Cloud expands hardened image support
item.description: Why hardened images are becoming the new baseline for container image security Container security is evolving beyond vulnerability scanning alone. Across the ecosystem - spanning container platforms, registries, and software supply chain tooling - customers are increasingly adopting hardened...
item.dc:creator: Yulia_Zhurbinsky

item.title: Inside ACR Artifact Cache: Pull-Through Caching at Scale
item.description: By: Akash Singhal, Luis Dieguez, Kiran Challa, Nathan Anderson, Tony Vargas, Caroline Barker, Ren Shao, Mabel Egba, Toddy Mladenov, Johnson Shi Introduction For many customers, Azure Container Registry (ACR) is the only registry their workloads can trust, even when images and artifacts originate...
item.dc:creator: akashsinghal

item.title: Is Your Monitoring Actually Working? What's New in Monitoring Coverage
item.description: Monitoring is only useful when the right signals are collected, the right alerts are in place, and the data is actually flowing when teams need it. In large Azure environments, confirming all three across every VM and AKS cluster can still take too much manual work. At Microsoft Ignite, we...
item.dc:creator: Nathan_Mangum

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Agent kit for Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: Your AI coding agents just got smarter about building high-performance database applications. The Azure Cosmos DB Agent Kit is now generally available, bringing proven best practices directly into your development workflow. Whether you're designing data m
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Introducing the Azure Functions serverless agents runtime (preview)
item.description: We're thrilled to announce the Azure Functions serverless agents runtime, now in public preview. It brings a new, markdown-first programming model for building AI agents as a first-class workload on Azure Functions, with the event-driven triggers, scale-to-zero economics, and operational...
item.dc:creator: AnthonyChu

item.title: Protect Azure Cosmos DB with vaulted backups using Azure Backup (preview)
item.description: As organizations increasingly rely on Azure Cosmos DB to power mission‑critical, globally distributed applications, protecting this data from accidental deletion, malicious activity, and ransomware has become more important than ever. At MS Build 2026, we’re excited to announce the preview of Azure...
item.dc:creator: shobhitgarg

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL updates for early-June
item.description: In early June 2026, the following update and enhancement was made to Azure SQL: Now you can use AES 256 symmetric keys to configure transparent data encryption (TDE) with customer-managed keys in Azure SQL Database. Learn more.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Secure, Modern Access to Azure Files on macOS with MS Entra ID
item.description: Azure Files now supports access from macOS using Entra ID authentication (Public Preview), enabling secure, identity-based access to file shares for cross-platform teams. This expands support for modern workflows, allowing users on macOS to seamlessly acc
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Foundry IQ: Improve recall by up to 54% with knowledge bases
item.description: By Alec Berntson, Jim Singh, Abhishree Shetty, Alina Stoica Beck, Amaia Salvador Aguilera, Arnau Quindós Sánchez, Thibault Gisselbrecht, Lihang Li, Jieyu Cheng, Jing Zhu, Mike Kim, Matt Gotteiner Agents are only valuable for the enterprise when they have access to your organization's knowledge. A...
item.dc:creator: MattGotteiner

item.title: What’s new in Observability at Build 2026
item.description: Observability for AI and Agent Workloads AI agents are moving from prototype to production. To help teams ship and operate agents with the same rigor as the rest of their stack, Azure Monitor & Azure Copilot Observability agent brings end-to-end agent observability — grounded in OpenTelemetry so...
item.dc:creator: Priyanka Nanda

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: File share centric management model (Microsoft.FileShares) for Azure Files
item.description: The new file share service management experience for Azure Files is now generally available for NFS 4.1 shares on SSD storage. Powered by the Microsoft.FileShares resource provider, file shares are now top-level Azure resources that you can create, secure
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Start Secure, Stay Secure: How Microsoft is Closing the Gap from Code to Runtime
item.description: Modern software applications composed of hundreds of artifacts, multiple programing languages, and cloud infrastructure provide an extensive attack surface that only continues to expand in the era of AI. According to Microsoft Threat Intelligence and industry research, threat actors are also...
item.dc:creator: JasonWeber

item.title: Managed Connectors for SRE Agent (preview)- Govern what your agent can do
item.description: Giving an agent access to a tool is the easy part. The harder question is what it's allowed to do with that access. "Can the agent copy a file in OneDrive?" mostly answers itself. "Can it copy any file, to any destination, over one that's already there?" is the one that decides whether the...
item.dc:creator: Dalibor_Kovacevic

item.title: Shaping what Azure SRE Agent does: Tool Permissions and Hooks
item.description: When an AI agent runs against production, the first question every security team asks is "What can it do, who decided it could, and what stops it from doing something it should not." Azure SRE Agent reached general availability in March. Since then, teams inside Microsoft and customers running it...
item.dc:creator: Dalibor_Kovacevic

item.title: Private Plugins with Azure SRE Agent
item.description: SRE's and platform teams are building operational skills specific to their infrastructure: investigation runbooks, compliance checks, cost analysis playbooks, deployment verification procedures. The next step is making that work reusable across every agent in the organization without exposing it...
item.dc:creator: ebencarek

item.title: Bring Your Own GitHub App: Connecting Azure SRE Agent to Enterprise Repositories
item.description: What if your SRE agent could access your enterprise GitHub repositories the same way your CI/CD pipelines do with a governed service identity, not a personal token? Azure SRE Agent connects to your GitHub repositories to build rich context about your systems source code, infrastructure definitions,...
item.dc:creator: dchelupati

item.title: Improved data processing features in Foundry IQ: Richer content extraction and data enrichment
item.description: Great answers start before retrieval. They depend on how well your data pipeline can reach enterprise content, preserve document structure, and carry text and visual context into the index. As part of the Microsoft Foundry platform, the Foundry IQ (Azure AI Search) 2026-05-01-preview release...
item.dc:creator: gia_mondragon

item.title: Azure Functions MCP Extension: What's New at Build 2026
item.description: The Azure Functions MCP extension has had a breakout year! Since its initial preview, the extension has grown from a single trigger type into a full-featured platform for building remote MCP servers: with tool, resource, and prompt triggers across multiple languages, MCP Apps for interactive UIs,...
item.dc:creator: lily-ma

item.title: [In development] Preview: Azure Cobalt 200 Arm-based Dpsv7, Dplsv7, Epsv7, Mpsv4, and Lpsv5-series Virtual Machines
item.description: The general purpose Dpsv7 and Dplsv7, memory-optimized Epsv7, high-memory Mpsv4, and dense storage Lpsv5 VMs are now available in preview. These virtual machines deliver significant enhancements over the previous generation Cobalt 100 VMs, offering improv
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Unified Text Translation API in Azure AI Translator
item.description: Azure AI Translator's unified Text Translation API reaches general availability, giving developers a single endpoint that fronts neural machine translation, large language model translation, small language model translation, Adaptive custom translation, a
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure API Management introduces a Unified Model API for multi-model AI applications.
item.description: As organizations adopt multiple LLM providers, developers often need to handle different API formats, SDKs, and integration patterns for each model vendor. This increases application complexity and makes provider switching, failover, and governance more d
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Anyscale on Azure: Powering Enterprise AI at Massive Scale on Azure Kubernetes Service
item.description: Somewhere on your AI platform team, an engineer is on call this weekend — not for the model, not for the training run, but for the integration code holding five separate AI processing systems together. Data preparation on one. Training on a second. Evaluation on a third. Serving on a fourth....
item.dc:creator: bobmital

item.title: Write Logic Apps in C#: introducing the Logic Apps Standard SDK
item.description: The workflow you always wished you could write in code If you build on Logic Apps Standard, you already know the deal: the runtime is excellent at the unglamorous parts of integration - connecting to systems, retrying, scaling, keeping run history you can actually debug. What you sometimes wanted...
item.dc:creator: WSilveira

item.title: New AI gateway capabilities in Azure API Management
item.description: Multi-model, multi-protocol AI applications are quickly becoming the norm. Teams are mixing OpenAI, Anthropic, and Vertex AI models, exposing tools through MCP, and wiring agents together with A2A. As that surface grows, so does the work of keeping it secure, observable, and consistent. Our ongoing...
item.dc:creator: budzynski

item.title: Announcing Go support in Azure Functions (Preview)
item.description: We're excited to announce that Azure Functions now supports Go as a first-class language, available today in public preview on the Flex Consumption plan. Go developers can now build event-driven, serverless applications using idiomatic Go, the standard toolchain they already love, and the full...
item.dc:creator: AnthonyChu

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure API Management Premium v2 and Standard v2 now support wildcard custom hostnames.
item.description: As API estates grow, managing individual custom domains and certificates for every subdomain can quickly become operationally complex and difficult to scale. Without wildcard support, every new subdomain — whether payments.api.contoso.com, inventory.api.c
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Global PTU Reservations Are Now Region-Agnostic
item.description: Global Provisioned Throughput (PTU) reservations are now region-agnostic, enabling a single reservation to cover Global PTU deployments across multiple regions. Why it matters:Optimize savings: One reservation can apply across regions, improving utilizati
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure Connector Namespaces: managed integration for any Azure compute
item.description: The integration tax nobody budgets for It is always a simple task on paper: connect apps to the systems the business actually runs on — SharePoint, Salesforce, SAP, Outlook — and get back to building features. What gets in the way is rarely the business logic. It's the plumbing. You write a custom...
item.dc:creator: WSilveira

item.title: Announcing Preview of Guest RDMA for Azure Boost
item.description: We’re excited to announce Guest RDMA (Preview) in Azure, beginning today in our UKSouth region, bringing high-throughput, ultra-low latency networking directly into guest virtual machines anywhere within the same region. By using Azure Boost to enable RDMA capabilities on our frontend network...
item.dc:creator: MengxiWu

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Backup for Cosmos DB
item.description: You can now enable vaulted backups for Azure Cosmos DB accounts, protecting mission-critical data with secure, isolated, and resilient backups. Azure Backup for Cosmos DB, now in preview, supports cyber-resiliency and compliance needs, even in scenarios i
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Policy Coverage for Model Router in Foundry Models
item.description: Azure Policy now provides centralized governance for deployments using the model router. Organizations can define and enforce routing standards across their environment, helping ensure that model selection aligns with security, compliance, and operational
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Voice Live integration with Microsoft Foundry Agent Service
item.description: Microsoft Foundry Agent Service is now generally available for Voice Live integration, allowing developers to connect real-time speech-to-text and text-to-speech to a Foundry Agent without writing their own audio pipeline. Existing agent capabilities — in
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Announcing Azure Linux 4.0: Purpose-Built for Azure, Now in Public Preview
item.description: Today at Microsoft Build, we're announcing the public preview of Azure Linux 4.0 - Microsoft's first party Linux distribution, purpose-built for Azure. Azure Linux 4.0 is available now for Azure Virtual Machines, VM Scale Sets, and container images – with Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) support and...
item.dc:creator: poorvinarang

item.title: Introducing Azure Container Linux (ACL)
item.description: Today at Microsoft Build 2026, we’re announcing the general availability of Azure Container Linux (ACL): a secure, immutable container host designed to help platform teams run Kubernetes workloads at scale on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) with greater consistency, reduced operational overhead, and...
item.dc:creator: FloraTaagen

item.title: Closing the AI-readiness gap with agentic modernization
item.description: Legacy debt is widening the AI-readiness gap Legacy systems and mounting tech debt aren't just slowing your AI agenda — they're quietly stealing its potential. Aging architectures, and complex, decades-old applications, databases, and infrastructure weren't designed for high performance, complex,...
item.dc:creator: Mike_Hulme

item.title: Azure Functions at Build 2026 Update
item.description: Azure Functions took another big leap at Build 2026. It is now the best programming model for event-driven apps and agents, on the best infrastructure to write secure code that scales. The headline features: serverless agents, connectors to M365, Teams, and more, Go, MCP, and Durable Tasks....
item.dc:creator: nzthiago

item.title: 🎉 Automation just became a team sport. Meet Azure Logic Apps Automation.
item.description: Low barrier to entry. Built for production. Now in Public Preview There's a moment that plays out in almost every organization right now. Someone closest a business problem - a retail ops lead, a finance analyst, a security analyst looks at a repetitive process and thinks, this should just run...
item.dc:creator: DivSwa
